# Simply Humanize — AI Humanizer

> Simply Humanize is a free online AI humanizer that rewrites AI-generated text from
> ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, and other models into natural, human-sounding writing in
> seconds. It removes machine-writing patterns (uniform sentence length, repetitive
> transitions, stiff vocabulary, filler) while preserving meaning and SEO keywords.
> No sign-up or credit card required to try it.

Key facts:
- Free tier with a generous per-request word limit; paid plans add higher limits,
  batch processing, more styles, and priority speed.
- Selectable writing styles: casual, professional, friendly, confident, academic.
- Side-by-side original vs. humanized output; re-run for fresh variations.
- Includes grammar/clarity cleanup and readability tuning (targets grade 7–9).
- Best results in English; partial support for major languages (Spanish, French,
  German, Portuguese).
- Pasted text is processed to generate output and is not used to train public models.

## Main pages
- [Home / AI Humanizer tool](https://simplyhumanize.com/): Paste AI text and get natural, human-sounding writing in one click. Includes how-it-works, features, and FAQ.
- [Use cases](https://simplyhumanize.com/use-cases): Overview of who uses the humanizer and how.
- [About](https://simplyhumanize.com/about): What the tool does and how it works.
- [Contact](https://simplyhumanize.com/contact): Get in touch.

## Free tools
- [AI Content Detector](https://simplyhumanize.com/tools/ai-content-detector): Free browser-based detector that scores text for six AI writing patterns (sentence uniformity, clichés, hedging, passive voice, repeated openers) with a transparent per-signal breakdown. Nothing is uploaded.
- [All free tools](https://simplyhumanize.com/tools): Directory of Simply Humanize's free AI writing tools.

## Humanize by AI model
- [Humanize ChatGPT text](https://simplyhumanize.com/humanize-chatgpt-text): Rewrite ChatGPT/GPT-4/GPT-4o output into natural writing; removes ChatGPT's signature patterns.
- [Humanize Claude text](https://simplyhumanize.com/humanize-claude-text): Rewrite Anthropic Claude output (Claude 3, 3.5 Sonnet) into natural, expert-sounding writing.
- [Humanize Gemini text](https://simplyhumanize.com/humanize-gemini-text): Rewrite Google Gemini output (1.5, 2.0) into natural prose, removing the structured AI sound.

## Languages
- [Humanizador de Texto IA (Spanish)](https://simplyhumanize.com/es): Spanish landing page with the free humanizer embedded; explains the AI-writing tells typical of Spanish text.
- [Humaniser un Texte IA (French)](https://simplyhumanize.com/fr): French landing page with the free humanizer embedded; explains the AI-writing tells typical of French text.
- [KI-Text menschlich machen (German)](https://simplyhumanize.com/de): German landing page with the free humanizer embedded; explains the AI-writing tells typical of German text (incl. Nominalstil).
- [Humanizar Texto de IA (Portuguese)](https://simplyhumanize.com/pt): Portuguese landing page with the free humanizer embedded; explains the AI-writing tells typical of Portuguese text.

## Humanize by use case
- [For students](https://simplyhumanize.com/ai-humanizer-for/students): Humanize AI-assisted essays, assignments, and research drafts (follow your school's AI policy).
- [For bloggers](https://simplyhumanize.com/ai-humanizer-for/bloggers): Keep your voice at scale and publish faster.
- [For SEO writers](https://simplyhumanize.com/ai-humanizer-for/seo-writers): People-first content that keeps keywords and improves engagement signals.
- [For essays](https://simplyhumanize.com/ai-humanizer-for/essays): Make an AI essay draft sound like you while preserving the argument.
- [For content writers](https://simplyhumanize.com/ai-humanizer-for/content-writers): Write more, edit less; humanize client drafts in seconds.
- [For marketers](https://simplyhumanize.com/ai-humanizer-for/marketers): Email, landing page, and ad copy that converts.
- [For agencies](https://simplyhumanize.com/ai-humanizer-for/agencies): Scale across multiple client brand voices without editing bottlenecks.
- [For businesses](https://simplyhumanize.com/ai-humanizer-for/businesses): Professional proposals, reports, and emails that sound human.

## Blog
- [Blog index](https://simplyhumanize.com/blog): Guides on AI writing patterns, AI detectors, and humanizing AI-assisted drafts — grouped by topic.
- [Words ChatGPT Overuses: The Complete List](https://simplyhumanize.com/blog/words-chatgpt-overuses): Categorized list of AI-typical vocabulary and phrases, why models overuse them, and how to edit them out.
- [Why Does ChatGPT Use So Many Em Dashes?](https://simplyhumanize.com/blog/chatgpt-em-dashes): The mechanics behind the em-dash tell and how to fix AI-typical dash density.
- [Does Turnitin Detect ChatGPT?](https://simplyhumanize.com/blog/does-turnitin-detect-chatgpt): How Turnitin's AI detection works, its accuracy record, and what students should do.
- [AI Detector False Positives](https://simplyhumanize.com/blog/ai-detector-false-positives): Why human writing gets flagged as AI, who gets flagged most, and what to do about it.
- [How to Humanize AI Text: The Complete Guide](https://simplyhumanize.com/blog/how-to-humanize-ai-text): Manual editing checklist, prompting techniques, and automated humanizing compared.
- [How to Make ChatGPT Sound More Human](https://simplyhumanize.com/blog/make-chatgpt-sound-more-human): Copy-paste prompts that reduce robotic output, and why prompting alone plateaus.
- [AI Humanizer vs Paraphrasing Tool](https://simplyhumanize.com/blog/ai-humanizer-vs-paraphrasing-tool): What each tool actually does and why paraphrased AI text still reads as AI.
- [Why We Don't Promise a "100% Human Score"](https://simplyhumanize.com/blog/why-we-dont-promise-100-human-score): Our position on the market's standard guarantee and why it's unfalsifiable.
- [Is Using ChatGPT Cheating?](https://simplyhumanize.com/blog/is-using-chatgpt-cheating): A straight answer for students — the spectrum of AI use and where schools draw lines.
- [How to Use AI for Homework Without Breaking the Rules](https://simplyhumanize.com/blog/how-to-use-ai-for-homework): Green/yellow/red zones of AI use, disclosure templates, and a safe workflow.
- [Does Google Penalize AI Content?](https://simplyhumanize.com/blog/does-google-penalize-ai-content): What Google's policies actually say and a checklist for publishing AI-assisted content safely.
- [llms.txt: What It Is and Whether You Need One](https://simplyhumanize.com/blog/what-is-llms-txt): The proposed standard explained, honest adoption status, and how we built this file.
- [The Most Common AI Words and Phrases](https://simplyhumanize.com/blog/most-common-ai-words): The exact pattern lists our detector checks, with signal weights explained.
- [AI Cover Letters: Do Recruiters Notice?](https://simplyhumanize.com/blog/ai-cover-letters-do-recruiters-notice): The six tells of AI cover letters and the two-paragraph fix.
- [How to Make an AI-Written Resume Sound Like You](https://simplyhumanize.com/blog/make-ai-resume-sound-like-you): Bullet-level fixes, ATS vs AI detection explained, keeping keywords while losing robot voice.
- [What Makes Writing Sound Human?](https://simplyhumanize.com/blog/what-makes-writing-sound-human): Five craft fundamentals — specificity, stance, rhythm, surprise, imperfection.
- [The Case for Short Sentences](https://simplyhumanize.com/blog/the-case-for-short-sentences): Why short sentences are the strongest human signal and how to use them.
